_README: PULSE INTEL — Live Forecast Tracking. This file is the source of truth for the Live Forecast Tracking card on the Dashboard. lock_prediction.py writes predicted_* fields and flips status to 'locked'. record_actual.py writes actual_* fields and flips status to 'resolved'. Mirror this file to https://github.com/pulse-intel-research/pulse-intel-predictions/blob/main/predictions.json
methodology_notes: Registry tracks both primary forecasts (v1-primary-2026) and general-election forecasts (v1-composite-tuned). Each prediction's per-race file specifies which methodology was used at lock time. Primary forecasts (KY, AL, CA, MI, MN) apply adjustments documented in PRIMARY_METHODOLOGY.md. General-election forecasts (NC, OH, ME) use the v1-composite-tuned ensemble in-distribution (the model was calibrated against 65 general elections, 2016-2024). Lock window: 10 days before primaries; ~30 days before generals (Oct 4, 2026 for Nov 3 races). No cherry-picking — all selected races are forecast publicly regardless of confidence.

predictions.notes: Open seat following McConnell retirement. 3-way GOP primary: Cameron, Barr, Morris. As of late April 2026, independent polls show Barr leading Cameron by ~5-7 points (Emerson Mar 29-31). Trump has not endorsed; endorsement could materially shift race.

Open seat — Tuberville running for governor. Trump endorsed Barry Moore Jan 17, 2026; post-endorsement polls show Moore leading Marshall ~+6. HIGH RUNOFF RISK on June 16 if no candidate clears 50% (likely given 47% undecided).

Newsom term-limited. Top-two nonpartisan primary; 61 candidates on ballot. Swalwell withdrew Apr 12, 2026 (sexual assault allegations). Post-Swalwell polling shows Hilton (R, Trump-endorsed), Becerra (D), Steyer (D), Bianco (R) all in 14-23% range. STRUCTURAL RISK: fragmented Dem field could allow 2 Republicans to advance to general.

Open seat after Peters retirement. Cook Toss-Up (general). Trump-endorsed Mike Rogers is decisive favorite on R side. Dem primary is genuinely competitive 3-way race: McMorrow vs El-Sayed (tied at 24% in Apr Emerson) vs Stevens (13%, declining). Generational split: under-40 favor El-Sayed, over-50 favor McMorrow.

Open seat — Tina Smith retiring (Klobuchar is running for GOVERNOR, NOT Senate). Dem primary is progressive-vs-moderate proxy fight: Peggy Flanagan (LG, Warren/Sanders-endorsed) vs Angie Craig (US Rep, DSCC-backed, AIPAC-friendly). Flanagan +13 in only public poll (GQR), but Craig has 4.7x cash advantage. R primary uncompetitive (R hasn't won statewide MN since 2006); top R candidates Royce White, Tafoya, Schwarze.

Open seat (Tillis retired). Roy Cooper (D, former 2-term governor, 6 prior statewide wins) vs. Michael Whatley (R, former RNC chair, never run for office). Cook Political Report moved to Lean D in April 2026 after Cooper led 14 pts in Catawba poll, 8 pts in HPU. PULSE INTEL Prediction Engine displays this race in the Active Forecast slot 1; methodology v1-composite-tuned applies in-distribution. Lock scheduled Oct 4, 2026 (~30 days pre-general).

Special election to fill JD Vance's seat. Sherrod Brown (D, former 3-term Senator) vs. Jon Husted (R, appointed incumbent). RCP avg: Husted +1 (essentially tied). Brown holds +7 net favorability vs Husted -3, but OH voted Trump +11 in 2024 and structural realignment with non-college voters favors Husted. PULSE INTEL Prediction Engine displays this race in slot 2; methodology v1-composite-tuned applies in-distribution. Lock scheduled Oct 4, 2026.

Susan Collins (R, 5-term incumbent) vs. presumptive D nominee from June 9 primary (Graham Platner +33 over Mills as of April; Mills rumored to be winding down). Collins net -19 favorability statewide, -30 with independents — worst polling of her career. BUT 2020 polling error precedent: polls had Collins -9, she won by +9 (18-pt swing). PULSE INTEL discounts polls accordingly. Cook: Toss-Up. Methodology v1-composite-tuned in-distribution. Lock scheduled Oct 4, 2026.
